titleStmt.title: Letter from William Dawson to the Resident Medical Superintendent of Kilkenny, 29 November 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Richard Dawson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Richard Dawson (1864-1950), the inspector of lunatics, to the Resident Medical Superintendent of Kilkenny. The letter is an instruction for the government to submit regulations to the asylum under section 9 sub section 6 of local Government Act (Ireland) 1898. William Richard Dawson was appointed HM inspector of lunatics in Ireland in 1911. During the Great War he served in the Royal Ambulance Medical Corps, gaining the rank of lieutenant-colonel, as a specialist in ‘nerve disease’ or shell shock to the troops in Ireland.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-1119-3

correspAction.placeName: The President Medical Superintendent, District Asylum, Kilkenny, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
29 November, 1915 Sir, I am directed by the Inspectors to state that
they have been instructed by Government to request
that your Committee will kindly submit Regulations
for the management of the Asylum in pursuance of secâ
tion 9 (subâsection 6) of the Local Government
(Ireland) Act, 1898. I am
Sir
Your Obedient Servant, The Resident Medical Superintendent District Asylum KILKENNY
